fourier_orbit_corollary.statement: For the raw full-family signed-e_m spectrum on D=alpha*mu_n, E((h^i t_i)_i)=E(t). On the primitive mode stratum every orbit has length n, hence PR(Rhat_prim)=n*PR(orbit amplitudes).
fourier_orbit_corollary.novelty_scope: Fourier-side participation-ratio specialization of the existing twist-orbit constancy/moment machinery; not a new discovery of twist symmetry.
fourier_orbit_corollary.masked_caveat: The same factorization for a masked residual requires the mask to be stable under M->hM. No such invariance is asserted for the current first-match residual.

scope_limits.reason: The toy has only 37248 nonzero modes, already below the deployed nu_ref=23088082660036. It tests spectral shape, exact flatness, and the w>=2/avg>>1 regime; it cannot certify the deployed inverse.

verdict: AUDIT/MEASURED: #414 already states the KB inverse. This packet adds the exact Fourier-side primitive twist-orbit PR factorization and the first w>=2, avg>>1 density/prefix-depth scaled KB subgroup toy. The toy is strongly flat and spectrally sparse, but its mass is mixed-primitive rather than monomial-led. The deployed signed-e_m inverse remains OPEN.
next_atomic_statement: After quotient routing, bound the participation ratio of the heavy primitive mixed twist-orbit amplitudes by at most 5772020665009/524288 at the Gamma2-1=1 reference, with an explicit compatible budget for any quotient contribution and with mask invariance proved if the masked residual is used.
nonclaims: does not prove U(1116048) <= Bstar or any deployed adjacent safe row
does not prove the raw or masked signed-e_m inverse at the deployed KB-MCA row
does not refute the monomial-collapse lemma; mixed-mode dominance is measured only at p=193,n=64,m=30,w=2
does not transfer the toy across subgroup index 3 to deployed index 1016
does not assert dual twist-orbit invariance for a first-match mask unless that mask is proved twist-stable
